name: Introduction to Computer Engineering and Computer Science
description: Introduction to the fields of computer engineering and computer science. Current and future trends and challenges in various fields of computing.

name: Introduction to Programming and Problem Solving
description: Introduction to basic concepts of computer science and fundamental techniques for solving problems using the Python programming language.

name: Digital Logic and Assembly Programming
description: Number representation, digital logic, Boolean algebra and logic gates, combinatorial building blocks, latches and flip-flops, arithmetic circuits, memory system organization, machine and assembly language programming, and MIPS architecture.

name: Discrete Structures with Computing Applications
description: Introduction to discrete mathematics with applications towards computing. Topics include sets, functions, logic, relations, graphs, trees, recursion, combinatorics, and mathematical reasoning.

name: Discrete Structures with Computing Applications II
description: Second course in computing applications of discrete structures. Topics include applications of computer arithmetic and matrices in computer systems. Programming assignments in Python.

name: Data Structures
description: Data structures including lists, hash tables, binary trees, balanced trees, heaps, sorting algorithms, graphs, external memory searching.

name: Object Oriented Application Development
description: Object oriented programming and design for large scale software. Class design, interfaces, inheritance, and polymorphism. Robust programming with exceptions, streams, iterators, and testing.

name: System Programming
description: Development, debugging, and deployment of systems-level software. Operating system abstractions for processes, memory management, file systems, and parallel programming. Modern C++ programming in a Unix-based environment.

name: Operating Systems
description: The structure and functions of operating systems. Interrupt handling, processes and interprocess communication, memory management, resource scheduling, information sharing and protection.
notes: Requires (CECS 275 or CECS 282 or CECS 325) AND (CECS 341 or CECS 346)

name: Introduction to Networks and Distributed Computing
description: Introduction to Distributed Computing and Interprocess Communication. Networking Protocols. Client Server Paradigm. Peer to Peer Networking. Sockets and the Socket API.

name: Algorithms
description: Greedy algorithms, dynamic programming, divide and conquer, network flow, approximation algorithms, NP-complete problems.

name: Concepts of Computer Science Theory
description: Fundamental topics in theoretical computer science. Regular languages, finite automata, context-free languages, Turing machines, computability theory, computational complexity, NP-completeness.

name: Computer Architecture and Organization
description: Basic computer architecture and organization, instruction set architecture, CPU, processor datapath, ALU, pipelining, memory systems, cache, virtual memory, I/O systems, multicore architectures.

name: Principles of Programming Languages
description: Fundamental topics in the design and implementation of programming languages. Programming language paradigms. Comparison of languages in type systems, data types, control flow, subroutines, concurrency, and exception handling.

name: Introduction to Software Engineering
description: Principles of software engineering, UML, modeling large software systems, requirements elicitation, object oriented analysis and design, introduction to design patterns, software testing, project management.

name: Introduction to Computer Security Principles
description: Fundamentals of cryptography and information and computer security. Basic concepts, theories and protocols. Software security, operating system security, database security, network security, digital forensics, privacy.

name: Stochastic Computing
description: Applications of probability and statistics to computing. Axioms of probability, random variables, discrete and continuous probability distributions, Chernoff bounds, randomized algorithms, Markov chains, Monte Carlo simulation, regression.
notes: Probability & Statistics requirement — choose CECS 381 or E E 381

name: Computer Science Senior Project I
description: First course in a two-course capstone design sequence. Design of a commercial grade software application including requirements analysis, functional, architectural and detailed design.

name: Computer Science Senior Project II
description: Second course in a two-course capstone design sequence. Implementation, testing, packaging and deployment of the system designed in CECS 491A.

name: Networks and Network Security
description: Examination of common computer security threats; fundamental techniques for implementing secure software systems and networks; practical experience in attacking and defending networked systems.

name: Database Fundamentals
description: Techniques for representing data using modern database systems. Designing schemas in relational, document, and other models of data storage. Data manipulation languages. Database application programming.
notes: Required for Software Development focus area

name: Selected Topics in Computer Science
description: Each offering is based upon an area of computer science and technology in which recent advances have been made.

name: Theory of Computation
description: Advanced topics in computability theory and computational complexity theory. Turing Machines, standard complexity classes P, NP, IP, PSPACE, hierarchy, decidability, and recognizability theorems.

name: Dynamic Networks
description: Fundamental concepts in dynamic networks. Graphs, random graphs, social and large-scale networks, network information, network theory, metrics, network algorithms and network models.
notes: Requires completion of at least 60 units

name: Analysis of Algorithms
description: Applications of standard combinatorial techniques to applied programming problems. Rigorous analysis of correctness/complexity of algorithms. Advanced graph algorithms, shortest paths, sorting, string matching, union find, divide-and-conquer.

name: Search Engine Technology
description: Models, algorithms, and evaluation of the retrieval of information from a collection of documents. Document preprocessing. Indexing and searching. Retrieval evaluation. Search engines.
notes: Requires CECS 328 AND (CECS 381 or MATH 380 or E E 381)

name: Software Project Management and Testing
description: Software project management concepts. Metrics, teams, estimation and scheduling, risks. Software testing, review techniques, quality assurance, testing strategies. Unit, integration, and regression testing.

name: Compiler Construction
description: Syntax directed compiler study. Organization of a compiler and overall design: parsing, semantic analysis, optimization and code generation.

name: User Interface Design
description: Evaluation, design and programming of user interface systems. Fundamentals of human cognition, system characteristics, and the interaction between humans and systems. Usability methods and user/task-centered design.

name: Computer Graphics
description: Introduction to the theory and practice of computer graphics. Graphics systems, 2-D and 3-D modeling, transformations, viewing transformations, projections, rendering techniques.

name: Data Visualization
description: Techniques in data visualization supporting the analytical process. General concepts, principles, and practices with specific visualization designs. Analytical techniques, interaction, and navigation design.

name: Artificial Intelligence
description: Introduction to the history and implementation of artificial intelligence agents. Search, constraint satisfaction, game-playing, logical agents, belief networks, optimal sequential decision systems.

name: Mobile Application Development
description: Languages and application programming interfaces for mobile device platforms. Development of thick and thin client applications for mobile devices.

name: Introduction to Game Programming
description: Introduction to game programming and graphics. Slow games. Realtime games with no adversary. Adversarial real-time games in 2-D.

name: Machine Learning
description: Introduction to machine learning and its applications, including supervised, unsupervised learning and deep learning. Regression, classification and neural networks.
notes: Required for Machine Learning & AI focus area. Requires CECS 381 or E E 381 or STAT 381

name: Applied Machine Learning
description: Fundamentals of machine learning with emphasis on applying techniques to real-world computing applications, including natural language processing, text mining, cybersecurity, and computer vision.

name: Deep Learning
description: Foundational and advanced deep learning concepts. Neural networks, backpropagation, specialized neural network architectures, deep reinforcement learning, and large language models.
notes: Requires CECS 381 or E E 381 or MATH 380

name: Hardware/Software Co-design
description: Introduction to top-down methods for hardware/software system-on-chip co-design. Design flow, system specification, software implementation, hardware synthesis, verification.

name: Web Programming and Accessibility
description: Introduction to World-Wide Web development. Accessibility issues. Web architecture, standards, and programming. Visual design principles and information architecture. Client-side and server-side programming.

name: Computer Network Interoperability
description: Overview of computer network theory and practice. Network infrastructure, LAN protocols, WAN protocols, switching technologies, IP, TCP, network security, configuration, design, and performance.

name: Software Development with Frameworks
description: Introduction to enterprise application development utilizing a modern software framework. Large-scale software design, framework components, data management, events, extensibility, user interfaces, web services.

name: Introduction to Data Security and Privacy
description: Fundamental concepts of data security, privacy, and cybersecurity with a focus on data science applications. Encryption, access control, network security. Regulatory compliance, privacy-preserving data analysis, ethical considerations.
notes: Required for Cybersecurity focus area. Requires CECS 328 or CECS 346

name: Introduction to Hardware Security
description: Principles of systems security from a hardware perspective. Vulnerabilities in digital systems, physical attacks, reverse engineering, counterfeit detection, hardware security primitives, secure IC design, hardware trojan detection, side-channel attacks.

name: Quantum Computation
description: Fundamental topics in quantum computation. Matrix formulation of quantum computation and information, Simon's Algorithm, Deutsch's algorithm, quantum circuits, Shor's factoring algorithm.

name: Computational Physiology
description: Introduction to cardiovascular and cerebrovascular systems and signals, and computational methods to analyze related signals and detect/predict physiological events.

name: Directed Studies
description: Assigned study in topics in current computer literature or computer-related projects with a final report.
notes: Requires consent of instructor. 1-3 units variable.
